_comment: Curated YouTube algorithm knowledge. Update manually when YouTube changes signals. Replaces fragile blog scraping.

pipeline_mechanics.browse_feed: Personalised recommendations on homepage. Driven by viewer watch history, topic affinity, and session signals. New channels enter via topic match, not subscriber count.
pipeline_mechanics.search: Keyword matching + engagement signals. Title, description, tags all indexed. Watch time after click is the strongest ranking signal for search results.
pipeline_mechanics.suggested: Sidebar and end-screen recommendations. Driven by co-watch patterns (viewers who watched X also watched Y). Most important discovery surface for small channels.

longform_insights: Videos over 8 minutes qualify for mid-roll ads — YouTube actively promotes content that enables this
AVD matters more than total watch time for algorithm ranking
Returning viewers signal high satisfaction — the algorithm weights repeat viewership heavily
First 30 seconds determine whether YouTube continues promoting a video
Session continuation (viewer watches another video after yours) is a strong positive signal
satisfaction_signals: Likes, shares, and comments relative to views
Survey responses (YouTube occasionally surveys viewers about satisfaction)
Depth of engagement (comments with substance vs. spam)
small_channel_notes: YouTube tests new channels via small audience samples. If the test group shows strong CTR + AVD, the algorithm expands reach. A 462-sub channel can reach millions if per-impression metrics are strong. Focus on CTR (thumbnail + title) and first-30-second retention.
avd_thresholds: No fixed percentage — relative to similar content in the topic niche. Aim for top quartile of similar-length videos in your topic.
ctr_thresholds: 4-10% is typical. Above 10% is exceptional. Below 2% signals thumbnail/title mismatch. CTR naturally decreases as reach expands beyond core audience.
sources_used: Todd Beaupré (YouTube discovery team) public talks
